title: FIQ Handler Example
description: Example FIQ handler demonstrating banked register usage and fast execution
code:
; FIQ Handler at 0x1C
FIQ_Handler:
 ; No need to save r8-r14 (already banked)
 SUB lr, lr, #4 ; Adjust return address
 ; Handle FIQ source
 LDR r0, =FIQ_SOURCE ; Get FIQ source register
 LDR r1, [r0] ; Read status
 CMP r1, #0 ; Check if FIQ pending
 BEQ FIQ_Exit ; Exit if no FIQ
 ; Clear FIQ and process
 MOV r2, #1
 STR r2, [r0, #0x20] ; Clear FIQ
 ; Process FIQ (user code here)
FIQ_Exit:
 SUBS pc, lr, #4 ; Return and restore CPSR
explanation: This FIQ handler demonstrates the advantage of banked registers. Since r8-r14 are banked in FIQ mode, no register saving is needed, reducing latency to minimum. The handler uses SUB to adjust return address and SUBS PC to restore CPSR from SPSR.

title: IRQ Handler with Nested Support
description: IRQ handler showing proper save/restore and enabling nested interrupts
code:
IRQ_Handler:
 SUB lr, lr, #4 ; Adjust return address
 STMFD sp!, {r0-r3, r12, lr} ; Save working registers
 ; Save SPSR for nested returns
 MRS r0, SPSR
 STMFD sp!, {r0}
 ; Check VIC for IRQ source
 LDR r0, =0xFFFFF000 ; VIC base address
 LDR r1, [r0, #0x00] ; IRQ status
 CMP r1, #0
 BEQ IRQ_End ; No IRQ pending
 ; Find and call specific handler
 BL Find_IRQ_Handler ; Get handler address
 MOV lr, pc
 BX r0 ; Call handler
 ; Enable nested IRQs
 CPSIE I ; Enable IRQ (nested)
 ; ... nested handler code ...
 CPSID I ; Disable IRQ before return
IRQ_End:
 LDMFD sp!, {r0} ; Restore SPSR
 MSR SPSR_cxsf, r0
 LDMFD sp!, {r0-r3, r12, pc}^ ; Restore and return
explanation: This IRQ handler shows complete context saving including SPSR for nested returns. It saves working registers, finds the specific IRQ source using VIC, calls appropriate handler, enables nesting for higher priority interrupts, then properly restores all state.

title: Exception Vector Table Setup
description: Setting up vector table in startup code
code:
; Vector Table (placed at 0x00)
AREA RESET, CODE, ATROOT
EXPORT __Vectors
__Vectors
 LDR pc, =Reset_Handler ; 0x00 Reset
 LDR pc, =Undef_Handler ; 0x04 Undefined
 LDR pc, =SWI_Handler ; 0x08 SWI
 LDR pc, =Prefetch_Handler ; 0x0C Prefetch Abort
 LDR pc, =DataAbort_Handler ; 0x10 Data Abort
 NOP ; 0x14 Reserved (for vectored IRQ)
 LDR pc, =IRQ_Handler ; 0x18 IRQ
 LDR pc, =FIQ_Handler ; 0x1C FIQ

; Handlers
Reset_Handler
 ; Initialize stack and jump to main
 LDR sp, =Stack_Top
 B Main

FIQ_Handler
 SUB lr, lr, #4
 ; FIQ processing here
 SUBS pc, lr, #4
explanation: This code sets up the ARM exception vector table. Each entry is a branch instruction to the respective handler. Note that 0x14 is reserved - this is where some systems put a vectored IRQ entry. The FIQ handler is at the last position allowing direct placement.

title: CPSR Interrupt Control
description: Demonstrating how to enable/disable interrupts using CPSR bits
code:
; Disable all interrupts
 CPSID I ; Set I=1, disable IRQ
 CPSID F ; Set F=1, disable FIQ
 CPSID IF ; Disable both IRQ and FIQ

; Enable interrupts
 CPSIE I ; Clear I=0, enable IRQ
 CPSIE F ; Clear F=0, enable FIQ
 CPSIE IF ; Enable both

; Read current interrupt state
 MRS r0, CPSR ; Move CPSR to r0
 ANDS r0, r0, #0xC0 ; Mask I and F bits
 BEQ interrupts_enabled ; If Z=1, interrupts enabled

; Using CPSR directly
 MRS r0, CPSR
 ORR r0, r0, #0x80 ; Set I bit to disable IRQ
 MSR CPSR_c, r0 ; Update CPSR (c = control field)
explanation: This shows different methods to control interrupt enable/disable in ARM. CPSID and CPSIE are preferred for atomic operations. The I bit (bit 7) disables IRQ, F bit (bit 6) disables FIQ. MRS moves from CPSR, MSR moves to CPSR with field specifiers (c=control, s=status, f=flags).
